docs.title: Welcome to the Corepedia
docs.text:
"Knowledge is all that survives." — StarCom Archival Directive 01

Welcome citizen to the official StarCom archival network.

The Corepedia is the largest surviving publicly accessible historical, technical, and political archive within civilized human space. Distributed through approved CoreNet terminals, relay hubs, educational institutions, and licensed vendor systems, this archive preserves the collective record of the Core and surrounding frontier regions.

docs.title: About the Archive
docs.text:
StarCom historians, navigators, military analysts, archivists, and intelligence specialists continuously maintain and expand this archive using recovered Empire-era records, government transmissions, navigational logs, battlefield reports, diplomatic archives, and verified civilian documentation.

The Corepedia contains records concerning:

  • The Great Houses and major political powers
  • Mercenary organizations and military commands
  • Interstellar conflicts and succession crises
  • Frontier regions and independent worlds
  • Military technologies, mech systems, and fleet doctrine
  • Historical figures, dynasties, and political leaders
  • StarCom communications and navigational history
  • Ancient Imperial infrastructure and recovered archives

docs.title: Content Disclaimer
docs.text:
Due to the fragmented nature of surviving historical records, certain entries may contain disputed information, contradictory accounts, or incomplete archival reconstruction. Many records originate from wartime transmissions, damaged Imperial databanks, conflicting state narratives, or restricted intelligence sources later cleared for limited public release.

StarCom does not guarantee complete historical accuracy for all archived material.

docs.title: Usage Warnings
docs.text:
Certain restricted entries remain unavailable to civilian users under current Conclave information security protocols.

Citizens are reminded that unauthorized tampering with archival records, restricted data caches, navigational libraries, or CoreNet relay infrastructure constitutes a violation of StarCom interstellar communications law and may result in suspension of network access, financial penalties, or criminal prosecution under applicable jurisdictional authority.

This archive endures so that civilization may remember.

StarCom — To Know the Future

docs.title: The Core
docs.text: “Civilization survives only so long as the Core endures.” — StarCom archival proverb

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Central Authority The Star Regency Current Star Regent Jacob Caledon Ruling House House Caledon Capital World Citadel Major Powers The Five Great Houses Current Era 5016 Words of House Caledon “Rule Above All.”

The Core refers to the central region of known human civilization and the political, military, industrial, and cultural heart of interstellar society.

The region is composed of several dozen densely populated and highly developed worlds governed collectively through the authority of the Star Regency and the alliance known as the Stellar Conclave.

Although commonly spoken of as a unified civilization, the Core is in practice a complex balance of competing powers, ancient traditions, military alliances, industrial interests, and political rivalries held together through diplomacy, mutual dependence, and the stabilizing authority of House Caledon.

While the Core remains the most prosperous and technologically advanced region of known space, tensions between the Great Houses continue to shape nearly every aspect of interstellar politics.

docs.title: The Great Houses
docs.text:
Five dominant powers comprise the political foundation of the modern Core:

Great House Common Name General Character Confederate Vanguard Union The Union Militarized industrial state emphasizing strength, discipline, and collective sacrifice Helios Sovereignty The Sovereignty Wealthy centralized power focused on stability, order, and economic influence Omnisphere Imperium The Imperium Ancient aristocratic realm preserving many old traditions of the Core Starcrest Protectorate The Protectorate Harsh frontier power built around resilience, honor, and martial duty Orion Corporate Orion Corporate Technological and scientific authority focused on innovation and internal development

Together these powers form the Stellar Conclave, the governing alliance of the Core Worlds.

Each maintains independent territory, militaries, economies, and political interests while recognizing the authority of the Star Regent and the necessity of preserving stability throughout the Core.

docs.title: The Star Regency
docs.text:
The Core is formally governed by the Star Regent from the capital world of Citadel.

The current Star Regent is Jacob Caledon of House Caledon, the ancient ruling line responsible for re-establishing civilization during the Great Restoration.

Though House Caledon controls comparatively little territory directly, its legitimacy and symbolic authority remain recognized throughout the Core Worlds.

The words of House Caledon are:

Rule Above All.

Among supporters of the Regency, these words are understood to mean that civilization, order, and stability must supersede the ambitions of any individual faction or ruler.

Critics occasionally argue that the Regency has become increasingly ceremonial in modern centuries, though few openly question the importance of preserving unity among the Great Houses.

docs.title: StarCom
docs.text:
No institution exerts greater infrastructural influence across the Core than StarCom.

Officially neutral and operating independently of the Great Houses, StarCom maintains: - communications relays - navigational archives - hyperspace routing systems - merchant traffic coordination - deep-space observation networks - strategic intelligence systems

The organization emerged during the early years following the Great Restoration and has since become indispensable to interstellar civilization.

Without StarCom, large-scale communication and coordinated governance across the Core would be impossible.

Its unofficial motto remains widely recognized throughout civilized space:

“To Know the Future, Trust in StarCom.”

docs.title: Civilization and Society
docs.text:
The Core contains the largest concentration of population, industry, military infrastructure, and scientific development in known space.

Most citizens of the Core live within: - massive planetary arcologies - industrial hive-cities - orbital habitats - heavily developed metropolitan worlds

Compared to the frontier regions beyond the Core, living standards are generally higher and political institutions more stable.

However, increasing tensions between the Great Houses, expanding frontier instability, piracy, economic rivalry, and political extremism continue to threaten the long-term stability of the region.

docs.title: Warfare
docs.text:
Modern warfare throughout the Core is dominated by: - mechanized combat - mercenary operations - limited territorial conflicts - strategic industrial warfare - covert operations - proxy engagements

Mechs form the backbone of nearly all modern ground combat forces.

Meanwhile, mercenary companies — commonly referred to throughout civilized space as “Metal Mercs” — play a major role in frontier security, deniable military operations, and inter-house conflicts.

Large-scale space warfare remains comparatively rare.

Many historians believe the technological and industrial capabilities required to sustain massive fleet warfare were partially lost during the Collapse and the long dark age that followed.

As a result, modern powers generally avoid direct fleet attrition whenever possible.

docs.title: History
docs.text:
Much of the Core’s earliest history has been lost.

According to surviving historical traditions, the Core was once united under a vast interstellar civilization commonly referred to simply as the Empire, though some records describe it as the Old Empire or the Golden Empire.

Stories from the era describe a civilization far more technologically advanced than the modern Core, though few surviving records can verify such claims.

At some point in the distant past, the Empire collapsed during a catastrophic conflict associated with the mysterious Ophidian Supremacy.

Very little reliable information concerning the Supremacy survives.

Some accounts describe the Ophidians as invaders from beyond known space. Others claim they were merely rival human civilizations, political fabrications, or mythologized interpretations of the Collapse itself.

No verified records from the era have ever fully confirmed the truth.

What is generally accepted is that the fall of the Empire was followed by a prolonged dark age during which much historical knowledge was lost and much of the Core fell into ruin.

Centuries later, the exiled remnants of the Empire are said to have returned from the deep fringe under the leadership of House Caledon during the conflict now known as the Great Restoration.

Following the Restoration, the modern Great Houses emerged, the Stellar Conclave was established, and the current era of Core civilization began.

Most surviving historical records originate from this post-Restoration period.

docs.title: Frontier Worlds
docs.text:
Beyond the Core lie vast frontier regions containing: - isolated colonies - mining worlds - pirate territories - mercenary enclaves - independent settlements - abandoned systems

While formally connected to the Core through trade and StarCom infrastructure, many frontier regions operate with limited oversight from the Great Houses.

Several important independent worlds exist outside direct Conclave authority.

Among the most notable are: - Drake’s Landing, the mercenary hub of civilized space - Titan Prime, the famed gladiator world

These worlds exert substantial influence over military culture, trade, and mercenary activity throughout the wider Core.

docs.title: History of the Core
docs.text: “History is not memory. History is what survives.” — StarCom Historical Directorate

docs.title: Historical Overview
docs.text:
Era Common Description The Age of Empire The ancient united civilization of humanity The Collapse The fall of the Empire and beginning of the dark age The Dark Age Occupation, fragmentation, and civilizational decline The Great Restoration Return of the Regent and reclamation of the Core The Modern Era Rise of the Great Houses and the Stellar Conclave

The history of the Core is fragmented, contradictory, and often mythologized.

Most surviving records originate only from the period following the Great Restoration. Earlier eras are known primarily through damaged archives, oral traditions, military legends, religious stories, and reconstructed StarCom historical records.

As a result, much of early Core history remains heavily disputed among historians.

Some scholars argue that many commonly accepted events may have been exaggerated, altered, or entirely fabricated over centuries of instability and record loss.

Nevertheless, a broad historical framework is generally accepted throughout civilized space.

docs.title: The Age of Empire
docs.text:
Long before the modern Great Houses existed, the Core is believed to have been united beneath a vast interstellar civilization commonly referred to simply as the Empire.

Some surviving traditions instead describe this period as: - the Old Empire - the First Empire - the Golden Age - the Golden Empire

No universally accepted name survives.

According to surviving accounts, the Empire controlled nearly all known Core space and possessed technological capabilities surpassing those of the modern era.

Stories from this period describe: - massive fleets - highly advanced scientific knowledge - stable interstellar governance - immense industrial worlds - near-unified human civilization

Very little verifiable evidence from this age survives intact.

Modern historians continue to debate how technologically advanced the Empire truly was, or whether many surviving stories have become exaggerated through centuries of retelling.

docs.title: The Collapse
docs.text:
At some point in the distant past, the Empire collapsed.

Most surviving traditions associate this collapse with the mysterious Ophidian Supremacy.

Descriptions of the Supremacy vary dramatically between surviving records.

Some accounts describe: - an external invading civilization - non-human conquerors - entities emerging from deep space - an empire from beyond the frontier

Other interpretations argue the Ophidians may have been: - rival human powers - a coalition of rebellious systems - a mythologized interpretation of civilizational collapse itself - political propaganda invented during the Restoration era

No verified evidence conclusively confirms the true nature of the Ophidian Supremacy.

What is generally accepted is that the Empire fell during a catastrophic period of warfare and instability that shattered interstellar civilization throughout the Core.

docs.title: The Dark Age
docs.text:
Following the Collapse, the Core entered a prolonged dark age.

Large portions of interstellar infrastructure failed or were abandoned. Many worlds became isolated from one another for generations. Scientific knowledge declined, centralized governance disappeared, and countless historical records were lost forever.

This period remains one of the least understood eras of human history.

Many regions devolved into: - isolated planetary states - warlord territories - pirate enclaves - collapsed industrial systems - fragmented frontier societies

Entire worlds vanished from surviving records.

Even basic interstellar travel became dangerous and unreliable across many regions.

docs.title: Survivors of the Dark Age
docs.text: While much of civilization collapsed, several powers survived in diminished forms.

docs.title: The Omnisphere Imperium
docs.text:
The Omnisphere Imperium is the only modern Great House widely acknowledged to have survived continuously from the time of the Empire itself.

Though the Imperium lost significant territory during the Collapse, its core worlds successfully endured through a combination of defensive isolation, fortified infrastructure, and strict internal control.

Imperium records claim their ancestors resisted the Ophidian Supremacy directly during the final years of the Empire.

Because of this continuity, the Imperium often presents itself as the oldest legitimate civilization remaining within the Core.

docs.title: The Starcrest Protectorate
docs.text:
The origins of the modern Starcrest Protectorate trace back to a larger pre-Collapse polity that did not survive the dark age intact.

The Protectorate itself emerged from the final defensive frontier regions of this fallen power.

Though greatly diminished, these surviving frontier defense forces managed to preserve their territory throughout the collapse and gradually expanded in strength over subsequent centuries.

This legacy continues to shape Protectorate culture, which strongly emphasizes resilience, sacrifice, and defensive duty.

docs.title: The Orion Cooperative
docs.text:
The organization that would eventually become Orion Corporate first emerged during the dark age as a defensive alliance between three isolated worlds attempting to survive the widespread instability of the era.

By pooling: - scientific expertise - industrial production - technological research - defensive resources

the Cooperative succeeded in preserving relative stability while much of the surrounding Core deteriorated.

Their intense focus on technological advancement developed largely as a reaction against the collapse they witnessed around them.

Over time, their superior technology and organizational efficiency allowed them to expand their influence substantially.

docs.title: The Exile
docs.text:
According to Restoration-era records, remnants of the Empire’s military forces survived the Collapse by fleeing beyond known Core space into the deep fringe.

Among these exiled survivors was the bloodline of House Caledon.

Very little verified information exists regarding the centuries spent in exile.

Some historians question whether the exile narratives are entirely literal, while others believe the returning forces may have originated from hidden frontier territories rather than true extra-Core exile.

Regardless of their origins, the return of the Caledon line would permanently reshape human civilization.

docs.title: The Great Restoration
docs.text:
Several centuries after the Collapse, the exiled Regent and the surviving Vanguard military forces returned to the Core during the conflict now known as the Great Restoration.

By this period, the mysterious Ophidian Supremacy had either vanished entirely or fragmented beyond recognition.

The returning Vanguard forces rapidly reclaimed much of the Core under the leadership of House Caledon.

The Great Restoration marked the rebirth of large-scale interstellar civilization.

Following the Restoration: - trade routes were re-established - jump networks stabilized - centralized governance returned - industrial systems recovered - communication networks expanded - surviving worlds were reunited

Most surviving modern historical records begin during this era.

docs.title: The Rise of the Great Houses
docs.text: The modern Great Houses largely emerged during the decades following the Great Restoration.

docs.title: The Confederate Vanguard Union
docs.text:
The Confederate Vanguard Union originated directly from the Regent’s Vanguard military forces during the Restoration.

As the returning armies reclaimed vast territories throughout the Core, House Caledon required a stable governing authority capable of administering these newly restored regions.

Statehood was therefore granted to the Vanguard military administration, eventually forming the Confederate Vanguard Union.

This origin continues to shape the Union’s identity as both a military state and a guardian of restored civilization.

docs.title: The Helios Sovereignty
docs.text:
The Helios Sovereignty emerged from the merchant houses, navigators, administrators, and economic coordinators who played a central role in rebuilding interstellar trade and civil infrastructure following the Restoration.

House Caledon relied heavily upon these organizations to: - restore safe travel - stabilize commerce - rebuild economic systems - coordinate reconstruction - re-establish interstellar governance

Through this process, the ruling Houses Aerin and Payne accumulated enormous wealth and political influence.

In modern centuries, critics occasionally argue the Sovereignty has drifted away from its original reconstruction ideals toward aristocratic excess and political elitism.

Nevertheless, Helios remains one of the primary stabilizing economic powers within the Core.

Members of Houses Aerin and Payne frequently serve as the Voice of the Regent — a highly prestigious office responsible for coordinating much of the practical governance of the Core Worlds.

docs.title: Orion Corporate
docs.text:
Following the Restoration, the technologically advanced Orion Cooperative aligned itself closely with the returning Regency.

Its reliability, industrial coordination, and scientific capabilities earned it increasing territorial authority and political influence.

Over time, the Cooperative evolved into the modern Orion Corporate.

docs.title: The Modern Era
docs.text:
The current era began following the establishment of: - the Star Regency - the Stellar Conclave - the Great Houses - StarCom - the restored Core trade networks

For several centuries, the Core has remained relatively stable compared to the chaos of the dark age.

However, tensions between the Great Houses continue to intensify.

Political rivalry, frontier instability, piracy, mercenary conflicts, industrial competition, and ideological division increasingly threaten the balance established during the Restoration.

Many historians believe the modern Core now stands at a critical turning point.

Whether civilization enters a new golden age or repeats the mistakes that once destroyed the Empire remains unknown.

docs.title: The Core
docs.text: “Civilization survives only so long as the Core endures.” — StarCom archival proverb

docs.title: The Star Regency
docs.text: “The Core endures because someone still holds the center.” — Common saying within Citadel

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Government Type Supranational Regency Current Star Regent Jacob Caledon Ruling Dynasty House Caledon Seat of Power Citadel Governing Alliance The Stellar Conclave Words of House Caledon “Rule Above All.”

The Star Regency is the central governing authority of the Core Worlds and the political institution responsible for maintaining unity among the Great Houses.

Ruled by House Caledon from the capital world of Citadel, the Regency serves as both the symbolic continuation of the ancient Empire and the stabilizing force holding modern interstellar civilization together.

Though the Great Houses maintain independent governments, militaries, territories, and political ambitions, all formally recognize the authority of the Star Regent and the legitimacy of the Regency.

In practice, the Regency functions less as a traditional empire and more as a system of managed balance between rival powers.

docs.title: House Caledon
docs.text:
House Caledon traces its lineage to the final years of the Empire.

According to Restoration-era historical records, the last Emperor fell defending the evacuation of the Empire’s surviving military forces during the Collapse. The precise details remain uncertain, though most surviving accounts describe a final defensive action carried out against the forces associated with the Ophidian Supremacy.

During the evacuation and subsequent exile beyond known Core space, leadership of the surviving fleet is believed to have passed to the ancestors of House Caledon under the title of Star Regent.

How long the exiled fleet remained beyond the Core remains unclear.

Most historians believe the exile lasted at least one generation, though some traditions claim it endured far longer.

Centuries later, House Caledon returned to the Core alongside the surviving Vanguard forces during the Great Restoration.

Following the reclamation of the Core Worlds, House Caledon established the modern Regency from Citadel and has ruled continuously ever since.

docs.title: Citadel
docs.text:
Citadel serves as the capital world of the Core and the seat of House Caledon.

The planet is regarded as politically neutral territory under direct Regency authority and functions as: - the center of Conclave governance - the residence of the Star Regent - the location of major diplomatic assemblies - the administrative heart of the Core

Citadel is among the most heavily defended worlds in known space.

Much of its infrastructure dates back to the earliest years following the Great Restoration, though portions of the world are believed to contain structures significantly older than the modern era.

docs.title: The Star Regent
docs.text:
The Star Regent serves as the supreme political authority of the Core Worlds.

Although the office does not directly govern the territories of the Great Houses, the Regent possesses extensive powers involving: - inter-house diplomacy - treaty enforcement - Conclave arbitration - emergency coordination - military oversight - preservation of interstellar law

The current Star Regent is Jacob Caledon.

Among both allies and rivals, Jacob is generally regarded as cautious, disciplined, and deeply committed to preserving stability throughout the Core.

Advanced age and declining health have increasingly shifted political attention toward the question of succession within House Caledon.

docs.title: The Stellar Conclave
docs.text:
The Stellar Conclave is the alliance of the five Great Houses under Regency authority.

The member powers are: - the Confederate Vanguard Union - the Helios Sovereignty - the Omnisphere Imperium - the Starcrest Protectorate - Orion Corporate

Each Great House maintains independent: - militaries - territorial governments - industrial economies - political systems

The Conclave exists primarily to: - prevent open war between the Houses - preserve trade and stability - coordinate large-scale policy - maintain the unity established during the Great Restoration

Political tensions between the Houses remain constant, however.

Competition over: - territory - influence - trade - technology - military prestige - frontier operations

continues to shape much of modern Core politics.

docs.title: The Voice of the Regent
docs.text:
Among the most prestigious political offices in the Core is the position known as the Voice of the Regent.

The Voice functions as the chief political administrator and primary executive representative of the Regency, overseeing much of the day-to-day governance of the Core Worlds.

Historically, members of Houses Aerin and Payne of the Helios Sovereignty have frequently served in this role due to their administrative and economic influence during the reconstruction of the Core following the Great Restoration.

In practice, the Voice often functions as: - chief diplomat - political coordinator - strategic advisor - administrator of Conclave affairs

while the Star Regent serves as the symbolic and stabilizing center of the Core itself.

docs.title: The Peace of the Core
docs.text:
The Regency officially forbids open warfare between the Great Houses.

In practice, maintaining peace across the Core remains extraordinarily difficult.

Proxy conflicts, covert operations, economic warfare, political sabotage, and mercenary engagements occur regularly throughout frontier regions and contested territories.

It is widely understood throughout the Core that the Great Houses frequently employ mercenary forces to advance political interests while avoiding direct confrontation.

Although the Regency officially condemns such practices, enforcement remains inconsistent.

Many political observers believe House Caledon tolerates limited indirect conflict because the alternative — open war between the Great Houses — would threaten the stability of civilization itself.

As a result, the Regency’s true role is often described not as preventing conflict entirely, but containing it within survivable limits.

docs.title: Relationship with StarCom
docs.text:
The Regency maintains extremely close ties with StarCom.

StarCom infrastructure enables: - interstellar governance - communications - navigational coordination - diplomatic operations - strategic intelligence gathering

Without StarCom support, the Regency could not effectively maintain authority across the Core Worlds.

Despite this relationship, StarCom officially remains politically neutral and institutionally independent from the Regency itself.

docs.title: Public Perception
docs.text:
Throughout the Core, perceptions of the Regency vary significantly.

Supporters view House Caledon as the final stabilizing force preventing humanity from descending once again into collapse and fragmentation.

Critics occasionally argue that the Regency has become overly bureaucratic, politically cautious, or incapable of adapting to growing instability across the frontier.

Even among critics, however, few openly advocate for the dissolution of the Regency itself.

The memory of the Collapse and the dark age that followed remains deeply embedded within Core political culture.

docs.title: Modern Outlook
docs.text:
As tensions between the Great Houses continue to intensify, the authority of the Regency faces increasing strain.

Frontier instability, growing militarization, economic rivalry, and political fragmentation have all placed new pressure upon the institutions established during the Great Restoration.

Whether House Caledon can continue preserving unity among the Great Houses may ultimately determine the future of civilization throughout the Core.

docs.title: Great Houses and Major Powers
docs.text:
“The Core does not survive through unity. The Houses fear open war more than they desire victory.” — StarCom political analysis archive

The modern Core Worlds are dominated by five Great Houses operating beneath the authority of the Star Regency. Though united under the Stellar Conclave, the Houses remain divided by ideology, culture, history, and competing visions for humanity’s future.

Alongside these powers stand major interstellar institutions whose influence rivals that of entire nations.

docs.title: Confederate Vanguard Union
docs.text:
"Forged From Victory."

A militarized industrial superpower built upon discipline, sacrifice, and overwhelming mechanized warfare. The Union believes civilization survives only through strength and unity. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

docs.title: Helios Sovereignty
docs.text:
"Wealth is Power."

A wealthy aristocratic state dominated by diplomacy, administration, finance, and political influence. The Sovereignty views itself as the civilized center of the modern Core. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

docs.title: Omnisphere Imperium
docs.text:
"Fierce Beauty."

The oldest surviving civilization in known space. The Imperium combines martial tradition, artistic excellence, and ancient cultural identity beneath the rule of House Lytherius. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

docs.title: Starcrest Protectorate
docs.text:
"Smoke and Steel."

A harsh frontier military state forged through survival during the Collapse. The Protectorate values endurance, sacrifice, discipline, and defensive warfare above conquest or luxury. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

docs.title: Orion Corporate
docs.text:
"For Futures Yet To Come."

The technological powerhouse of the Core. Orion Corporate believes civilization survives only through scientific advancement, rational governance, and continued progress. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

docs.title: The Star Regency
docs.text:
"Rule Above All."

The central governing authority of the Core Worlds under House Caledon. The Regency exists to preserve stability and prevent open war between the Great Houses. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

docs.title: StarCom
docs.text:
"To Know the Future."

The neutral communications and navigation authority that maintains the infrastructure of interstellar civilization itself. Without StarCom, the modern Core could not function. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}

docs.title: Political Landscape
docs.text:
The balance between these powers defines nearly every major conflict, alliance, trade agreement, proxy war, and frontier crisis throughout the Core Worlds.

Although open war between the Great Houses is officially forbidden under Regency law, political rivalry remains constant. Competition over territory, industrial capacity, technological superiority, trade influence, and frontier influence continues to shape the modern era.

As a result, Mercenaries flourish throughout the Core and frontier regions alike. Mercenary companies are frequently employed by all major Houses to conduct raids, border conflicts, security operations, deniable offensives, and proxy wars without risking direct military escalation between House governments.

This system of controlled indirect conflict has become one of the defining political realities of the modern Core.

docs.title: Confederate Vanguard Union
docs.text:
“We are not soldiers. We are conquerors.” — Ryder Hawkins

State propaganda illustration depicting Victor Hawkins (center) and his children, Maximus (left) and Ryder (right) Hawkins, standing before the military strength of the Confederate Vanguard Union. Images of this kind are commonly displayed throughout Confederate space to symbolize unity, strength, and reverence for House Hawkins.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Government Type Military State Capital Hawkins Leadership Confederate Military Council Primary Strength Industry and Mechanized Warfare Military Specialty Heavy Mech Assault National Motto “Forged From Victory.”

The Confederate Vanguard Union, commonly referred to simply as the Union or the Confederates, is one of the five dominant powers of the Core Worlds.

The Union is widely regarded as the most militarized state in the Core and possesses the largest standing mech force among the Great Houses. Its society is heavily shaped by military service, industrial labor, and state-directed nationalism.

Union culture emphasizes strength, sacrifice, discipline, and collective identity above individual ambition. Service to the state is regarded not merely as duty, but as the foundation of civilization itself.

While critics often describe the Union as authoritarian and expansionist, supporters argue that the Confederates possess the resolve and industrial capacity necessary to preserve humanity against the growing instability of the frontier.

docs.title: History
docs.text:
The origins of the Confederate Vanguard Union trace back to the collapse of the Golden Empire and the wars that followed.

During the chaos of the Succession Wars and the occupation of the Core by the Ophidian Supremacy, remnants of the Empire’s Vanguard military forces continued fighting long after many other regions had collapsed.

These surviving military commands eventually regrouped around the industrial world of Hawkins.

Following the Great Purge and the destruction of the Ophidian Supremacy, these military remnants formed the foundation of the modern Confederate Vanguard Union.

As a result, the Union continues to view itself not merely as another successor state, but as the rightful heir to the military traditions of the old Empire.

docs.title: Hawkins
docs.text:
Hawkins is the capital world of the Confederate Vanguard Union and one of the most heavily industrialized planets in the Core.

The planet is dominated by: - military foundries - mech assembly complexes - orbital shipyards - industrial arcologies - defense manufacturing zones

Much of Hawkins is dedicated to sustaining the Union’s military infrastructure.

The world is also home to the Honor Guard, one of the oldest surviving military organizations in the Core.

docs.title: Government
docs.text:
The Union is governed through a centralized military state structure overseen by the Confederate Military Council.

Military leadership exerts enormous influence over political, industrial, and social policy.

Citizenship within the Union is closely tied to service, and military veterans occupy many positions of authority throughout Confederate society.

The Union justifies this system as necessary to preserve unity, discipline, and preparedness in an increasingly dangerous galaxy.

Opponents frequently criticize the Confederates for suppressing dissent and prioritizing military expansion over civilian freedoms.

Union officials generally dismiss such criticism as political rhetoric originating from weaker states unwilling to make difficult sacrifices for long-term survival.

docs.title: Society and Culture
docs.text:
Confederate society is built around: - military service - industrial productivity - sacrifice - loyalty to the state - collective identity

Public life within the Union heavily glorifies: - soldiers - industrial workers - mech pilots - military heroes - historical victories

State propaganda frequently portrays the Union as the shield protecting humanity from chaos, weakness, and fragmentation.

The phrase:

Forged From Victory.

is commonly displayed throughout Confederate territory and reflects the Union belief that strength and unity are born through struggle.

docs.title: Military
docs.text:
The Confederate Vanguard Union possesses the largest mech force in the Core Worlds.

Confederate doctrine emphasizes: - overwhelming mechanized assaults - industrial warfare - sustained battlefield pressure - heavy armor - direct engagement - logistical endurance

Union mechs are widely known for: - durability - battlefield reliability - ease of repair - high heat tolerance - mass-production efficiency

Confederate commanders generally favor decisive force over subtle maneuvering.

The Union also maintains extensive armored divisions, artillery forces, orbital support fleets, and mechanized infantry formations.

docs.title: The Honor Guard
docs.text:
The Honor Guard is among the most prestigious military organizations within the Union.

Originally formed from surviving Vanguard veterans during the wars against the Ophidian Supremacy, the Honor Guard has become both a ceremonial and elite combat formation.

Membership within the Guard is considered one of the highest honors attainable in Confederate society.

Throughout the Core, the Honor Guard possesses a reputation for: - discipline - battlefield effectiveness - loyalty - unwavering resolve

docs.title: Frontier Policy
docs.text:
The Union maintains an aggressive presence throughout frontier regions bordering the Core.

Confederate leadership argues that instability beyond the Core inevitably threatens civilization itself and therefore justifies proactive military intervention.

As a result, Confederate forces are frequently involved in: - anti-piracy campaigns - frontier security operations - industrial protection contracts - territorial disputes - mercenary suppression actions

Rival powers often accuse the Union of using “security operations” to expand its territorial influence.

The Confederates reject these accusations and maintain that they are simply willing to confront threats other states prefer to ignore.

docs.title: Relations with Other Powers
docs.text:
The Union maintains especially tense relations with the Helios Sovereignty, whose aristocratic culture and economic dominance are often criticized within Confederate propaganda.

Relations with the Starcrest Protectorate fluctuate between cooperation and rivalry depending upon frontier conditions.

The Confederates remain wary of Orion Corporate influence, particularly regarding advanced technology and private industrial monopolies.

Despite political tensions, the Union remains a formal member of the Stellar Conclave and recognizes the authority of the Star Regent.

docs.title: Reputation
docs.text:
Throughout the Core, the Confederate Vanguard Union is viewed as: - disciplined - militaristic - industrial - resilient - uncompromising

Supporters regard the Confederates as the strongest defenders of humanity remaining in civilized space.

Critics view them as increasingly authoritarian and dangerously expansionist.

Even among rivals, however, Confederate military strength is rarely underestimated.

docs.title: Modern Outlook
docs.text:
As tensions rise throughout the Core and instability spreads across frontier space, the Confederate Vanguard Union continues to expand its military readiness and industrial production.

Confederate leadership maintains that another great conflict between the powers of the Core is inevitable.

Whether the Union ultimately becomes humanity’s shield or the architect of another devastating interstellar war remains one of the defining political questions of the modern era.

docs.title: Helios Sovereignty
docs.text:
“Strength wins battles. Wealth wins wars.” — Arthur Payne

Archon Alissa Aerin and Lord Arthur Payne standing united at the center of the Helios Sovereignty, surrounded by the heirs and bloodlines of Houses Aerin and Payne. From left to right stand Archer Payne, Torrin Payne, Alexander Payne, Alissa Aerin, Arthur Payne, Elara Aerin, Lex Aerin, Ravin Aerin, and Aurora Aerin, with the towering war machines of the Sovereignty looming behind them.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Government Type Aristocratic Sovereignty Ruling Houses House Aerin and House Payne Capital World New Terra Population 18.7 billion Primary Strength Commerce, administration, and diplomacy :material-lion: Common Nickname The Lions State Philosophy “Order Through Prosperity.”

The Helios Sovereignty is one of the five Great Houses of the Core and among the wealthiest and most politically influential powers in known space.

The Sovereignty is ruled jointly by the aristocratic Houses Aerin and Payne, whose families played a critical role in rebuilding interstellar civilization following the Great Restoration.

During the chaotic decades after the return of House Caledon and the Vanguard forces, the emerging Regency relied heavily upon Helios merchants, navigators, financiers, administrators, and industrial coordinators to restore: - trade - safe interstellar travel - taxation systems - communications infrastructure - economic stability - large-scale governance

As a result, the Sovereignty became deeply intertwined with the administrative foundation of the modern Core.

Centuries later, Helios influence extends throughout nearly every major institution of civilized space.

The Sovereignty is known throughout the Core for: - wealth - sophistication - diplomacy - advanced technology - political influence - aristocratic culture

Critics frequently describe the Helios elite as arrogant, decadent, and increasingly detached from the hardships of frontier life.

Supporters counter that without the stability created by the Sovereignty, the modern Core itself would not exist.

docs.title: History
docs.text:
The Helios Sovereignty emerged during the early years following the Great Restoration.

As House Caledon reclaimed territory throughout the Core, immense challenges emerged involving: - trade coordination - reconstruction - navigation - industrial recovery - taxation - governance - civilian logistics

Merchant houses, navigators, financiers, and administrative coalitions centered around Houses Aerin and Payne became indispensable to this process.

Through their efforts, interstellar commerce and civil administration gradually stabilized across the recovering Core.

The wealth generated during this reconstruction period elevated the emerging Sovereignty into one of the dominant powers of the new era.

Over the centuries, this wealth and influence gradually transformed the culture of the Sovereignty itself.

What began as a practical administrative alliance evolved into one of the most aristocratic and politically influential societies in the Core.

docs.title: Government
docs.text:
The Sovereignty is jointly ruled by House Aerin and House Payne from the capital world of New Terra.

Political authority within Helios is distributed through: - aristocratic councils - ministerial offices - economic directorates - trade authorities - noble administrative families

Compared to several other Great Houses, the Sovereignty places enormous emphasis upon: - diplomacy - governance - economic management - political influence - institutional stability

While elections and regional representation exist in limited forms, real political influence remains concentrated among the upper aristocratic and administrative classes.

docs.title: The Voice of the Regent
docs.text:
The Helios Sovereignty maintains unusually close ties with the Star Regency.

Historically, members of Houses Aerin and Payne have frequently served as the Voice of the Regent — one of the most prestigious political offices in the Core.

The Voice acts as: - chief political administrator - diplomatic coordinator - strategic advisor - executive representative of the Regency

In practice, many observers regard the Voice as responsible for much of the day-to-day governance of the Core Worlds.

This longstanding relationship has granted the Sovereignty enormous political influence within Conclave affairs.

Critics occasionally argue that Helios influence over the Regency has become excessive.

Supporters counter that the Sovereignty’s administrative expertise remains essential to maintaining civilization-wide stability.

docs.title: New Terra
docs.text:
New Terra is the capital world of the Helios Sovereignty and one of the wealthiest planets in the Core.

The planet is famous for: - massive arcologies - advanced infrastructure - luxury districts - financial centers - universities - administrative complexes - orbital trade stations

New Terra represents the height of Core prosperity and sophistication.

At the same time, critics throughout the frontier often view the world as symbolic of the growing distance between the Core elite and ordinary citizens living beyond its borders.

docs.title: Society and Culture
docs.text:
Helios culture strongly values: - refinement - education - status - diplomacy - achievement - political sophistication

The Sovereignty places enormous emphasis upon etiquette, presentation, and social standing.

Public life among the aristocracy is dominated by: - political galas - formal ceremonies - economic negotiations - patronage networks - elite academic institutions

The Sovereignty also possesses some of the most advanced educational systems in the Core.

Helios citizens frequently occupy influential positions throughout: - commerce - diplomacy - administration - finance - scientific research

Despite its polished public image, social inequality within the Sovereignty has become increasingly pronounced in recent centuries.

docs.title: Military
docs.text:
Although not as openly militarized as the Confederate Vanguard Union, the Helios Sovereignty maintains highly advanced military forces.

Sovereignty doctrine emphasizes: - precision strikes - battlefield coordination - advanced electronics - tactical flexibility - mobility - command superiority

Helios mechs are widely known for: - advanced targeting systems - elegant design - superior battlefield networking - high-quality engineering

Compared to the heavy industrial philosophy of the Union, Helios forces generally favor precision and technological sophistication over brute force.

docs.title: Diplomacy and Politics
docs.text:
The Sovereignty is widely regarded as the diplomatic center of the Core.

Helios politicians and negotiators play major roles in: - Conclave diplomacy - trade arbitration - inter-house negotiations - economic coordination - frontier policy

The Sovereignty often portrays itself as the civilized and stabilizing force preventing the Core from descending into militarism and chaos.

Rival powers sometimes interpret this position as arrogance or political manipulation.

docs.title: Relations with Other Houses
docs.text:
The Sovereignty maintains especially tense relations with the Confederate Vanguard Union.

Union propaganda frequently portrays the Helios elite as decadent aristocrats disconnected from ordinary citizens and overly dependent upon wealth and privilege.

In turn, Helios commentators often describe the Union as militaristic, crude, and dangerously authoritarian.

Relations with Orion Corporate are generally cooperative due to shared economic and technological interests, though competition between the two powers remains significant.

The Sovereignty also maintains relatively stable diplomatic relations with House Caledon and the Star Regency due to centuries of political cooperation.

docs.title: Reputation
docs.text:
Throughout the Core, the Helios Sovereignty is viewed as: - wealthy - sophisticated - influential - refined - politically powerful - technologically advanced

Its supporters view it as the foundation of modern civilization and the primary force preserving order and prosperity throughout the Core.

Its critics view it as increasingly elitist, politically manipulative, and detached from the realities facing frontier populations.

Even among rivals, however, few dispute the Sovereignty’s immense influence over modern interstellar society.

docs.title: Modern Outlook
docs.text:
The Helios Sovereignty continues to position itself as the administrative and economic center of the modern Core.

As instability spreads throughout frontier space and tensions between the Great Houses intensify, Helios leadership increasingly argues that only strong centralized coordination and enlightened governance can preserve civilization from fragmentation.

Whether the Sovereignty ultimately represents humanity’s greatest achievement in stability and prosperity — or the slow decadence of an overconfident elite — remains one of the defining political questions of the modern era.

docs.title: Omnisphere Imperium
docs.text:
“When the stars went dark, the Imperium carries the flame of civilization.” — Imperial remembrance inscription

State propaganda illustration depicting Imperator Jada Lytherius (center) flanked by her children: Taikario Lytherius (left), Sylara Lytherius (lower left), Valtoria Lytherius (right), and Rythor Lytherius (lower right), standing before the military and cultural power of the Omnisphere Imperium. Towering war machines, dragon banners, and the spires of the Imperial Core rise behind the ruling bloodline of House Lytherius.

docs.title: :material-dragon: Overview
docs.text:
Government Type Imperial aristocracy Ruling House House Lytherius Current Ruler Imperator Jada Lytherius Capital World Celestia Prime Population 10.3 billion Military Strength 220,000 mechs Friendly Ties Starcrest Protectorate Hostilities Orion Corporate; Confederate Vanguard Union Words “Fierce Beauty.”

The Omnisphere Imperium, often called the Imperium or the Dragon Riders, is one of the five Great Houses of the Core and the oldest surviving state in known human civilization.

The Imperium is widely recognized as the only surviving power from the age of the ancient Empire. Its roots extend even further back, to the centuries before the Empire united the Core, when the region that would become the Imperium was divided among smaller states known for metallurgy, engineering, and fierce competition over rare minerals and energy sources.

In the modern Core, the Imperium is known for honor, duty, artistic excellence, military discipline, and a cultural philosophy that joins beauty with strength. Its words, “Fierce Beauty,” reflect this union of refinement and martial power.

The Imperium also maintains a deep symbolic association with fire. Fire represents civilization, continuity, memory, life, and the will to endure. Imperial tradition often describes House Lytherius and the Imperium as the keepers of the flame that survived the Collapse and carried civilization through the dark age.

docs.title: History and Origins
docs.text:
The history of the Imperium begins centuries before the rise of the ancient Empire.

Before its formation, the region that would become the Imperial heartland was fragmented among multiple smaller powers. These states possessed distinct cultural practices, but many shared a focus on metallurgy, engineering, and resource extraction. Frequent conflicts over rare minerals and energy sources shaped the early military and industrial traditions of the region.

The rise of House Lytherius is traditionally tied to Vyrexia Lyther, a brilliant and ambitious leader remembered as both a skilled warrior and a tactical visionary.

The defining moment in early Imperial history was the development of the first war mech. This breakthrough granted House Lytherius a sudden and overwhelming battlefield advantage, allowing rapid expansion across surrounding territories and laying the foundation for the Omnisphere Imperium.

The Imperium’s early conquests funded both military and artistic growth. Wealth from conquered territories supported the industrial base necessary to maintain mech armies, while also patronizing artists, poets, engineers, philosophers, and scholars.

This fusion of martial supremacy and artistic achievement became one of the defining features of House Lytherius culture.

docs.title: The Old Empire and the Collapse
docs.text:
At its height, the early Imperium commanded vast territories and possessed unmatched technological superiority through its mech armies.

Over time, however, the scale of its territory became a vulnerability. Internal rebellions, political betrayal, economic strain, and external pressures weakened Imperial dominance.

Eventually, the broader ancient Empire emerged, and the reduced but still powerful Omnisphere Imperium became a member state within that larger order.

During the Collapse and the dark age that followed, the Imperium endured where nearly every other major power fell.

Imperial records claim the Imperium resisted the forces associated with the Ophidian Supremacy and preserved its core territories through fortified defense, discipline, and strict internal cohesion.

Although the Imperium lost worlds during the era, its government, ruling house, and central culture survived intact.

This continuity remains central to Imperial identity.

docs.title: Darker History
docs.text:
The modern Imperium often emphasizes honor, art, beauty, and stability, but StarCom historical records also preserve accounts of a harsher early Imperial past.

Early Imperial expansion was frequently brutal.

Defeated forces were often given grim ultimatums: swear loyalty or face execution. In several accounts, defiant captives were annihilated immediately using mech weaponry as a demonstration of Imperial power.

The early Imperium also relied upon slave labor to accelerate industrialization across conquered worlds. Dissenters, rebels, and military prisoners were often publicly executed to suppress resistance.

Whole populations were relocated into strategic settlements, local cultures were disrupted, and Imperial dominance was displayed through grand military parades featuring war trophies and captives.

Blood sports involving prisoners also formed part of early Imperial spectacle, serving both as entertainment and as a reminder of state power.

The modern Imperium has formally abandoned the most brutal of these practices. However, this darker history is often softened, romanticized, or minimized within later Imperial accounts.

docs.title: Celestia Prime
docs.text:
The capital of the Omnisphere Imperium is Celestia Prime, known throughout the Core as the “Jewel of the Imperium.”

Celestia Prime is a world of striking natural and architectural beauty, marked by: - towering mountains - sprawling forests - vast oceans - advanced military facilities - traditionally inspired art and architecture - major centers of Imperial culture

The planet’s economy thrives on both advanced technology and artistic production.

Celestia Prime embodies the Imperial ideal of “Fierce Beauty”: a world where refinement, military power, natural grandeur, and historical continuity exist side by side.

docs.title: Ruler and Leadership
docs.text:
The Imperium is ruled by House Lytherius from Celestia Prime.

Its current ruler is Imperator Jada Lytherius, widely regarded as wise, stoic, careful, and politically measured.

Under Jada’s leadership, the Imperium favors calculated action over impulsive escalation. It is slow to react when provoked, but always prepared to defend itself with formidable force when necessary.

House Lytherius traditionally favors consorts over marriage in order to preserve the ruling family lineage. Jada Lytherius has four children with at least two separate regional leaders from within the Imperium.

In Lytherian tradition, children of the ruling line are regarded as belonging to the Imperium itself. Their primary familial bond is therefore with the Imperator and the state, rather than with their fathers.

Jada’s children are: - Takarion Lytherius - Valtorian Lytherius - Sylara Lytherius - Rythor Lytherius

Takarion and Valtorian, the two eldest sons, are respected military tacticians and serve among the Imperium’s top commanders.

Sylara Lytherius is known as a diplomatic prodigy. Her preference for peaceful solutions sometimes places her at odds with more martial members of her family.

Rythor, the youngest, is drawn more directly to combat and continues to refine his skills as a mech pilot.

docs.title: Society and Culture
docs.text:
Imperial culture places great value on: - honor - duty - artistic excellence - discipline - beauty - martial skill - historical continuity

Many Imperial citizens are trained in artistic pursuits as well as practical or military disciplines.

The Imperium’s ideal citizen is not merely a warrior, scholar, engineer, or artist, but someone who brings perfection to whatever role they occupy.

This emphasis on perfection in all endeavors is one of the central features of Imperial culture.

docs.title: The Eternal Flame
docs.text:
Fire occupies a central spiritual and cultural role within the Imperium.

Imperial philosophy associates flame with: - civilization - continuity - knowledge - sacrifice - memory - human survival

Many Imperial ceremonies involve: - great braziers - torch processions - eternal flames - ceremonial burning rites - fire-lit military vigils

This symbolism is closely tied to the Imperium’s belief that it preserved the last surviving flame of civilization during the dark age while much of the Core descended into ruin.

The dragon imagery commonly associated with the Imperium derives partly from this philosophy. Dragons represent both destructive power and the guarded fire of civilization itself.

docs.title: Politics and Diplomacy
docs.text:
The Imperium employs a deliberate and cautious political approach.

Its leaders prefer measured responses to threats and challenges rather than hasty escalation. When provoked, however, the Imperium responds with calculated resolve and formidable force.

The Imperium maintains friendly ties with the Starcrest Protectorate, as both powers share cultural foundations rooted in honor and duty.

Relations with the Orion Corporate and the Confederate Vanguard Union are strained.

Orion Corporate often views the Imperium as traditional and outdated, too bound to ritual and inherited customs.

The Union views the Imperium as a serious military contender, particularly because Imperial military strength challenges the Union’s claim to military supremacy.

Relations with the Helios Sovereignty are generally reasonable, largely because geography limits direct conflict between them rather than because of deep cultural similarity.

docs.title: Military and Technology
docs.text:
Imperium mechs possess a distinctive non-humanoid, serpentine style. This design tradition contributes to the Imperium’s popular nickname: “the Dragon Riders.”

Imperial battlefield doctrine emphasizes: - agility - speed - hit-and-run attacks - precision ambushes - heat damage - overwhelming opening volleys

Although many Imperium mechs are lightly armored, they are extremely fast within their weight classes.

Their signature technology is the advanced arcanite engine, which grants above-average speed and mobility.

This mobility allows Imperial forces to strike quickly, disengage, and re-engage on favorable terms.

docs.title: Blasters and Heat Warfare
docs.text:
One of the Imperium’s most distinctive weapon systems is the Blaster, a burst-damage laser weapon.

Blasters accumulate and store multiple charges, then discharge them simultaneously during an attack. This allows a single opening volley to strike with two or three times the force of a conventional laser burst.

Blasters are useful in prolonged battles, but they are especially dangerous during the first moments of an engagement, when Imperial forces can unleash concentrated fire and then withdraw to recharge.

Imperium mechs also frequently use: - High Energy Cannons - Torchers - heat-focused weapon systems

These weapons force opponents to manage overheating, internal damage, and mobility constraints.

The Imperium exploits these weaknesses through superior speed, precise ambushes, and controlled disengagement.

docs.title: Mercenary Relations
docs.text:
Mercenaries often find the Imperium difficult to work for at first.

The Imperium does not believe in rewards without trust. New mercenary companies may struggle financially while attempting to prove their reliability and earn Imperial confidence.

However, those who gain the trust and respect of the Imperium often find it to be one of the most generous employers in the Core.

As a result, mercenaries who successfully establish themselves with the Imperium are often fiercely loyal to its cause.

docs.title: Public Reputation
docs.text:
Throughout the Core, the Omnisphere Imperium is viewed as: - ancient - honorable - beautiful - disciplined - aristocratic - militarily formidable - culturally refined

Its admirers view it as a living remnant of humanity’s lost greatness.

Its critics see it as proud, rigid, and overly attached to mythologized traditions.

Even among critics, however, the Imperium’s survival through the dark age and its role in the development of war mechs grant it a historical stature few powers can rival.

docs.title: Modern Outlook
docs.text:
The Omnisphere Imperium remains one of the most stable and respected powers in the Core.

It is no longer the dominant civilization it once was, yet it has endured longer than any other known state.

As tensions rise between the Great Houses, the Imperium continues to present itself as the keeper of civilization’s flame: ancient, disciplined, beautiful, and prepared to defend what remains of the Core.

docs.title: Orion Corporate
docs.text:
“They who control the present, control the future.” — Orion Corporate doctrine

Corporate propaganda illustration depicting Prodirector Deckard Noddack standing at the center of the Orion Corporate Directorate alongside his wife, Dr. Alise Noddack (right), Zyce Noddack-Voss (left) head of the Corporate Internal Security Forces, and Celeste Noddack (far right) the daughter and heir of the Corporate. Behind them loom the sleek cyclopean mechs of the Orion Corporate, their glowing mono-eye sensors watching over the technological heart of the Core.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Government Type Corporate-Technocratic Alliance Capital World Orion Prime Founding Structure The Three Pillars Population 12.4 billion Primary Strength Technology and Scientific Advancement Military Specialty Advanced Electronics and Precision Warfare National Motto “For Futures Yet To Come.”

Orion Corporate is one of the five Great Houses of the Core and the foremost technological power in known space.

Unlike the ancient aristocracies and military states that dominate much of the Core, Orion Corporate emerged during the dark age itself as a cooperative alliance dedicated to preserving knowledge, infrastructure, and civilization during a time of widespread collapse.

Today, Orion is known throughout the Core for: - scientific advancement - technological superiority - industrial efficiency - meritocratic governance - advanced computing systems - engineering excellence

Supporters regard Orion Corporate as humanity’s greatest engine of progress and innovation.

Critics frequently accuse Orion of arrogance, technocratic detachment, and placing efficiency above tradition and culture.

Regardless of political perspective, few dispute Orion’s immense influence over modern technology throughout the Core.

The Orion national motto, “For Futures Yet To Come,” reflects the organization’s long-term civilizational philosophy: that humanity must continue advancing, innovating, and building not merely for the present generation, but for those still to come.

docs.title: History
docs.text:
The origins of Orion Corporate trace back to the dark age following the Collapse.

As much of the Core fragmented into isolation, piracy, and industrial decline, three surviving worlds formed a mutual defense and resource-sharing alliance in order to preserve stability and technological continuity.

This alliance eventually became known as the Three Pillars.

By combining: - scientific research - industrial production - engineering expertise - logistical coordination - defensive infrastructure

the Three Pillars succeeded in preserving a relatively advanced and stable civilization while much of the surrounding Core deteriorated.

Witnessing the collapse of interstellar civilization profoundly shaped Orion philosophy.

Where many surviving powers turned inward or embraced rigid traditionalism, Orion concluded that civilization could survive only through: - technological advancement - rational governance - scientific progress - coordinated cooperation

Over time, the growing alliance expanded economically and territorially, eventually evolving into the modern Orion Corporate.

docs.title: The Great Restoration
docs.text:
When House Caledon and the Vanguard forces returned during the Great Restoration, Orion Corporate was already one of the few functioning multi-world powers remaining in the Core.

The returning Regency relied heavily upon Orion technological infrastructure and engineering expertise during the reconstruction of civilization.

Through close cooperation with the Regency, Orion gained major political legitimacy and increasing territorial responsibility throughout the recovering Core.

Many historians believe the Restoration could not have succeeded as rapidly without Orion support.

docs.title: Government
docs.text:
Orion Corporate is governed through a complex technocratic corporate structure composed of: - executive directorates - scientific councils - industrial boards - administrative sectors - research authorities

Unlike the hereditary aristocracies of several other Great Houses, Orion places enormous emphasis upon merit, expertise, education, and technical achievement.

Political authority is strongly tied to: - competence - scientific contribution - industrial leadership - administrative performance

This meritocratic structure has contributed significantly to Orion’s technological success.

Critics, however, argue that Orion governance can appear cold, bureaucratic, and detached from ordinary citizens.

docs.title: Orion Prime
docs.text:
Orion Prime serves as the capital world of Orion Corporate and one of the most technologically advanced planets in known space.

The planet is famous for: - research megacities - industrial arcologies - orbital manufacturing platforms - advanced universities - automated logistics systems - scientific institutes

Large portions of Orion Prime are devoted to research and development.

Compared to many Core worlds, Orion Prime emphasizes efficiency, infrastructure, and technological integration over ceremonial grandeur or aristocratic aesthetics.

docs.title: Society and Culture
docs.text:
Orion society strongly values: - education - innovation - expertise - efficiency - discipline - scientific advancement

Education systems throughout Orion are among the most advanced in the Core.

Citizens showing exceptional talent in: - engineering - mathematics - physics - logistics - computing - industrial sciences

are often rapidly elevated into influential institutional roles.

Compared to many other Great Houses, Orion culture tends to be less ceremonial and more utilitarian.

This contributes to the widespread perception that Orion citizens are: - highly intelligent - efficient - emotionally reserved - intensely competitive

docs.title: Technology
docs.text:
Orion Corporate possesses some of the most advanced technology in the modern Core.

The organization is especially famous for: - advanced computing systems - precision electronics - targeting systems - battlefield networking - industrial automation - reactor engineering - sensor technology

Many technologies considered standard throughout the modern Core were originally developed or refined by Orion researchers.

The House’s focus on scientific progress stems directly from its origins during the dark age, when technological decline nearly destroyed civilization itself.

docs.title: Military
docs.text:
Orion military doctrine emphasizes: - precision warfare - advanced battlefield coordination - electronic superiority - mobility - sensor dominance - tactical flexibility

Orion mechs are widely regarded as among the most technologically sophisticated in the Core.

They frequently incorporate: - advanced targeting systems - superior sensors - enhanced battlefield networking - experimental weapons platforms - highly optimized reactors

Unlike the brute-force military philosophy of the Confederate Vanguard Union, Orion forces generally prioritize efficiency, coordination, and precision.

docs.title: Relations with Other Houses
docs.text:
Orion Corporate maintains especially strained relations with the Omnisphere Imperium.

Imperial traditionalists frequently criticize Orion for: - excessive technological dependence - utilitarian governance - cultural sterility - disregard for tradition

In turn, many within Orion view the Imperium as overly rigid and resistant to necessary progress.

Relations with the Helios Sovereignty are generally cooperative due to shared economic and administrative interests, though competition between the two powers remains significant.

The Confederate Vanguard Union remains wary of Orion’s technological superiority and strategic influence.

docs.title: Reputation
docs.text:
Throughout the Core, Orion Corporate is viewed as: - advanced - efficient - intelligent - meritocratic - technologically dominant - politically calculating

Supporters view Orion as humanity’s best hope for continued progress and long-term survival.

Critics often regard the House as detached, elitist, and increasingly willing to subordinate culture and tradition to efficiency and technological advancement.

Even among rivals, however, Orion’s scientific and industrial achievements command enormous respect.

docs.title: Modern Outlook
docs.text:
As instability spreads throughout the Core and tensions between the Great Houses intensify, Orion Corporate increasingly argues that civilization’s survival depends upon continued technological advancement and rational coordination.

Many Orion leaders believe humanity cannot afford another dark age.

For Orion leadership, technological progress is not viewed as ambition alone, but as obligation.

The phrase:

“For Futures Yet To Come.”

appears throughout Orion space on government buildings, research facilities, educational institutions, and military installations alike.

To Orion citizens, the motto represents the belief that civilization survives only when humanity continues building toward the future rather than retreating into fear, stagnation, or nostalgia.

For this reason, Orion continues investing heavily in: - scientific research - industrial expansion - advanced computing - military innovation - exploration beyond the frontier

Whether Orion Corporate ultimately becomes the architect of humanity’s future — or a civilization increasingly disconnected from its own humanity — remains a growing subject of debate throughout the Core Worlds.

docs.title: The Star Regency
docs.text:
“Rule above all.” — Words of House Caledon

House Caledon, the ruling lineage of the Stellar Conclave upon Citadel. Seated upon the Throne of the Core is the Star Regent Jacob Caledon, flanked by his heir apparent and eldest son to the left alongside the heir’s wife and children. To the Regent’s right stands his younger brother and his brother's wife, beside his own son. Honor Guard troops and ceremonial war mechs stand watch within the Hall of Ascension beneath the banners of the Conclave.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Government Type Supranational Regency Current Star Regent Jacob Caledon Ruling Dynasty House Caledon Seat of Power Citadel Governing Alliance The Stellar Conclave Words of House Caledon “Rule Above All.”

The Star Regency is the central governing authority of the Core Worlds and the political institution responsible for maintaining unity among the Great Houses.

Ruled by House Caledon from the capital world of Citadel, the Regency serves as both the symbolic continuation of the ancient Empire and the stabilizing force holding modern interstellar civilization together.

Though the Great Houses maintain independent governments, militaries, territories, and political ambitions, all formally recognize the authority of the Star Regent and the legitimacy of the Regency.

In practice, the Regency functions less as a traditional empire and more as a system of managed balance between rival powers.

docs.title: House Caledon
docs.text:
House Caledon traces its lineage to the final years of the Empire.

According to Restoration-era historical records, the last Emperor fell defending the evacuation of the Empire’s surviving military forces during the Collapse. The precise details remain uncertain, though most surviving accounts describe a final defensive action carried out against the forces associated with the Ophidian Supremacy.

During the evacuation and subsequent exile beyond known Core space, leadership of the surviving fleet is believed to have passed to the ancestors of House Caledon under the title of Star Regent.

How long the exiled fleet remained beyond the Core remains unclear.

Most historians believe the exile lasted at least one generation, though some traditions claim it endured far longer.

Centuries later, House Caledon returned to the Core alongside the surviving Vanguard forces during the Great Restoration.

Following the reclamation of the Core Worlds, House Caledon established the modern Regency from Citadel and has ruled continuously ever since.

docs.title: Citadel
docs.text:
Citadel serves as the capital world of the Core and the seat of House Caledon.

The planet is regarded as politically neutral territory under direct Regency authority and functions as: - the center of Conclave governance - the residence of the Star Regent - the location of major diplomatic assemblies - the administrative heart of the Core

Citadel is among the most heavily defended worlds in known space.

Much of its infrastructure dates back to the earliest years following the Great Restoration, though portions of the world are believed to contain structures significantly older than the modern era.

docs.title: The Star Regent
docs.text:
The Star Regent serves as the supreme political authority of the Core Worlds.

Although the office does not directly govern the territories of the Great Houses, the Regent possesses extensive powers involving: - inter-house diplomacy - treaty enforcement - Conclave arbitration - emergency coordination - military oversight - preservation of interstellar law

The current Star Regent is Jacob Caledon.

Among both allies and rivals, Jacob is generally regarded as cautious, disciplined, and deeply committed to preserving stability throughout the Core.

Advanced age and declining health have increasingly shifted political attention toward the question of succession within House Caledon.

docs.title: The Stellar Conclave
docs.text:
The Stellar Conclave is the alliance of the five Great Houses under Regency authority.

The member powers are: - the Confederate Vanguard Union - the Helios Sovereignty - the Omnisphere Imperium - the Starcrest Protectorate - Orion Corporate

Each Great House maintains independent: - militaries - territorial governments - industrial economies - political systems

The Conclave exists primarily to: - prevent open war between the Houses - preserve trade and stability - coordinate large-scale policy - maintain the unity established during the Great Restoration

Political tensions between the Houses remain constant, however.

Competition over: - territory - influence - trade - technology - military prestige - frontier operations

continues to shape much of modern Core politics.

docs.title: The Voice of the Regent
docs.text:
Among the most prestigious political offices in the Core is the position known as the Voice of the Regent.

The Voice functions as the chief political administrator and primary executive representative of the Regency, overseeing much of the day-to-day governance of the Core Worlds.

Historically, members of Houses Aerin and Payne of the Helios Sovereignty have frequently served in this role due to their administrative and economic influence during the reconstruction of the Core following the Great Restoration.

In practice, the Voice often functions as: - chief diplomat - political coordinator - strategic advisor - administrator of Conclave affairs

while the Star Regent serves as the symbolic and stabilizing center of the Core itself.

docs.title: The Peace of the Core
docs.text:
The Regency officially forbids open warfare between the Great Houses.

In practice, maintaining peace across the Core remains extraordinarily difficult.

Proxy conflicts, covert operations, economic warfare, political sabotage, and mercenary engagements occur regularly throughout frontier regions and contested territories.

It is widely understood throughout the Core that the Great Houses frequently employ mercenary forces to advance political interests while avoiding direct confrontation.

Although the Regency officially condemns such practices, enforcement remains inconsistent.

Many political observers believe House Caledon tolerates limited indirect conflict because the alternative — open war between the Great Houses — would threaten the stability of civilization itself.

As a result, the Regency’s true role is often described not as preventing conflict entirely, but containing it within survivable limits.

docs.title: Relationship with StarCom
docs.text:
The Regency maintains extremely close ties with StarCom.

StarCom infrastructure enables: - interstellar governance - communications - navigational coordination - diplomatic operations - strategic intelligence gathering

Without StarCom support, the Regency could not effectively maintain authority across the Core Worlds.

Despite this relationship, StarCom officially remains politically neutral and institutionally independent from the Regency itself.

docs.title: Public Perception
docs.text:
Throughout the Core, perceptions of the Regency vary significantly.

Supporters view House Caledon as the final stabilizing force preventing humanity from descending once again into collapse and fragmentation.

Critics occasionally argue that the Regency has become overly bureaucratic, politically cautious, or incapable of adapting to growing instability across the frontier.

Even among critics, however, few openly advocate for the dissolution of the Regency itself.

The memory of the Collapse and the dark age that followed remains deeply embedded within Core political culture.

docs.title: Modern Outlook
docs.text:
As tensions between the Great Houses continue to intensify, the authority of the Regency faces increasing strain.

Frontier instability, growing militarization, economic rivalry, and political fragmentation have all placed new pressure upon the institutions established during the Great Restoration.

Whether House Caledon can continue preserving unity among the Great Houses may ultimately determine the future of civilization throughout the Core.

docs.title: StarCom
docs.text: “Civilization survives through communication.” — StarCom Foundational Doctrine

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Organization Type Interstellar Communications and Navigation Authority Operational Reach Core Worlds and Frontier Regions Primary Headquarters Classified Primary Responsibilities Communications, Navigation, Infrastructure Coordination Political Alignment Neutral under the Star Regency Official Motto “To Know the Future, Trust in StarCom.”

StarCom is the primary communications, navigation, and interstellar coordination authority of the Core Worlds.

Operating independently of the Great Houses under the protection of the Star Regency, StarCom maintains the infrastructure necessary for large-scale civilization throughout known space.

Its responsibilities include: - interstellar communications - navigational routing - hyperspace telemetry - merchant traffic coordination - relay maintenance - archival preservation - strategic observation - deep-space monitoring
